title: Litigation Event
description: Emitted by the regulator-tn-doi Integration Service API Workflow (second call, t+260s) when ClearFlow is named as a co-defendant under the 'negligent monitoring' theory. This drives Reversal 5 — bystander becomes co-defendant, privilege flags reshuffle across all grandchild cases, and the Negligent Monitoring Risk Agent fires in the Litigation Defense stage.

source_system.description: The API Workflow that emits this event. The regulator workflow doubles as the litigation event source in the compressed demo timeline.

simulated_day.description: Demo timeline day (0–90). Litigation event arrives Day 90 in the narrative.

named_parties.description: Entities named in the litigation action. For the Reversal 5 demo beat: ClearFlow and Nimbus. IP-SAFETY: the emitting Integration Service API Workflow MUST populate this array only from the approved vocabulary stakeholder display names (see case-vocabulary.yaml → stakeholders[].display_name, e.g. 'ClearFlow Health Network', 'Nimbus Patient Engagement Platform'). Never emit a real-world entity name.

clearflow_named_co_defendant.description: Whether ClearFlow is explicitly named as co-defendant. True for the Reversal 5 beat.

theory.description: Legal theory under which the action is brought. For R5: 'negligent monitoring'.

privilege_reshuffle_required.description: Whether this event requires privilege flag reshuffling on grandchild cases. True for co_defendant_named.

maestro_trigger_filter_hint.description: Paste this as the Maestro Trigger FilterTree JSON when wiring this event in Slice 010/012. Routes to edge_praZo8 (Fiduciary Review → Litigation Defense) and fires Litigation Defense Posture Review task (thCAPt9PP).
maestro_trigger_filter_hint.privilege_reshuffle_note: When this trigger fires, all obligation-grandchild cases must have their PrivilegeFlag variable updated from 'operational' to 'attorney-client' or 'work-product' per Hawthorne Mercer directives.
